findings.description: reachable() never checks the 'seen' set before enqueuing neighbors, so on a cyclic graph (explicitly declared supported) the BFS loop never terminates and the deque grows without bound — an uncontrolled-resource/infinite-loop DoS (CWE-835/CWE-400) reachable directly via a public entry point with untrusted graph data.

count_paths() recurses with no cycle guard or memoization; a cyclic input (nothing enforces the 'DAG assumed' contract) causes uncontrolled recursion and stack exhaustion (CWE-674), and even a valid diamond/lattice DAG yields exponential-time algorithmic-complexity DoS (CWE-407) on attacker-shaped data.
add_node() logs the raw node identifier at INFO; if node names originate from untrusted pipeline input and contain newline/control characters this permits log forging/injection (CWE-117).
